Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.content: Looking at the image, the figure has changed shape again. The red blocks are on the left side. Let me check the segmentation.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('W', 'N', 'R', 'G'):
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"{n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
print("\nTotal green:", sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N'))
print("Total red:", sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
